title: Non-Amazon scraping wrap-up: Premcurve, Lulus, Petal & Pup

messages.text: Continue the non-Amazon scraping work while coordinating with concurrent Codex chats to avoid duplicate site work.
Checked active scrape claims before selecting sites. Avoided already claimed or blocked targets and updated claim files as each site was started or completed.
Do the premcurve.com full catalog scrape.

Completed Premcurve using full public Shopify catalog discovery plus the public Judge.me aggregate media feed. Final output had 1,054 rows, 355 distinct reviews, 1,054 images, 103 product URLs, and 68 rows qualified with image, product URL, size, and measurement. Recorded the caveat that exhaustive per-product fallback was impractical because the site repeatedly returned the same Judge.me aggregate media rows and many zero-row probes.

Checked and documented Barse, Goelia, and Melanie Lyne outcomes. Barse was skipped as out-of-scope jewelry. Goelia had 1,761 public apparel products but no public review feed rows. Melanie Lyne had 5,592 catalog products and public Yotpo review endpoints, but sampled reviewed products exposed no customer review image fields.

Selected Lulus because it was unclaimed and had only legacy nonstandard data. Built scrape_lulus_reviews.py. Live Lulus Nuxt review-page parsing worked initially, but local command-line fetches began returning PerimeterX captcha/403. Converted the existing Lulus workbook bigImages and oldFormat review-photo sheets into the shared Step 1 intake schema instead. Final Lulus output had 4,117 review-image rows, 22 distinct product URLs, and 2,322 qualified rows. Synced outputs and reports to S3 and verified with a dry run.
Answered that Lulus had 2,322 qualified rows using the standard definition: image URL plus product URL plus size plus at least one measurement/profile stat.

Selected Petal & Pup as an unclaimed zero-row merchant. Built scrape_petalandpup_reviews.py using public Shopify catalog data and public Yotpo widget JSON. A 20-product smoke produced 471 rows and 249 qualified. Full catalog attempts hit rate limits: products.json returned HTTP 429 on page 4, and sitemap/product .js metadata discovery also hit HTTP 429. Completed a bounded partial scrape from products.json pages 1-3: 750 products discovered, 705 apparel products scanned, 947 review-image rows, 126 products with image rows, and 688 qualified rows. Marked the claim and summary as partial_catalog_products_json_pages_1_3 and still needing full scrape after cooldown.

Regenerated non-Amazon merchant scrape summary reports. Synced Petal & Pup, Lulus, reports, claim files, and then the full FWM_Data directory to s3://fwm-scraping-data-briannasinger using non-destructive aws s3 sync --size-only. Final full S3 dry-run verify showed no pending changes.
This chat thread is done. Update the git, S3, and the transcripts table.
Prepared final wrap-up: S3 synced and verified; Git scoped to this thread's scraper and documentation artifacts; transcript JSON prepared for upload to the Supabase codex_chat_transcripts table.

blockers: Lulus local command-line live fetches returned PerimeterX captcha/403, so final output uses workbook review-photo conversion.
Petal & Pup full catalog attempts hit HTTP 429 on products.json page 4 and product .js metadata crawling; final output is a bounded partial scrape.
